reasoning: The Federal Reserve has currently held interest rates steady at 4.25%-4.50% through its May 7, 2025 meeting. Because the resolution date for this question is July 1, 2025, the only remaining opportunity for a rate cut is the FOMC meeting scheduled for June 17–18, 2025. Following the May meeting, market futures significantly reduced the likelihood of a June cut. The CME FedWatch Tool showed an ~80% probability that the Fed would hold rates steady in June, effectively implying only a 20% chance of a rate cut. Although the April CPI report released on May 13 showed inflation cooling slightly, concerns regarding upcoming tariffs are causing the Fed to remain cautious, pushing consensus expectations for the first rate cut into July or later. Thus, the probability closely aligns with the market-implied odds of ~22%.
